creator: pauls@utdallas.edu
text:
--On August 12, 2008 11:18:42 AM +0900 Tomoyuki Sakurai 
<cherry@trombik.org> wrote:

> On Mon, Aug 11, 2008 at 08:14:41PM -0400, Wesley Shields wrote:
>>
>> I noticed that Paul (pauls@utdallas.edu) is the maintainer of this new
>> bro-devel port.  Is this intentional or was it a mistake left over from
>> the bro port?  If it is a mistake I'll gladly fix it so you are the
>> maintainer.  If Paul is supposed to be the maintainer I will need his
>> approval to commit this as he will be the one responsible for it.
>>
>
> It's, sort of, intentional. security/bro has been broken for a while.
> It's so broken that fixing it without user's surprise is difficult (yes,
> I tried). The upcoming release is the best opportunity to fix the issues.
>
> If he would fix the port and/or maintain security/bro-devel, that's fine
> with me. However, the maintainer is not using it personally[1]. If
> someone steps up as a maintainer, great. I'm not willing to, but if no
> one cares, I will.
>

That is correct.  I was asked to fix the security/bro port because it was 
out of date.  Since doing that, I have discovered that bro is much more 
complex than my port allows for.  So, I began working on 
security/bro-devel, but Chris Lee, who was working with me on some 
patches, volunteered to take it over.  I don't know what, if anything, he 
has done with the port since.  The port requires someone who not only uses 
bro and understands its intricacies but can also deal with source code 
patching to get the software to work on FreeBSD.

If you want to take both ports over, I will gladly give them up.  I don't 
think I'm qualified to maintain them.

Paul Schmehl (pauls@utdallas.edu)
Senior Information Security Analyst
The University of Texas at Dallas
http://www.utdallas.edu/ir/security/
